description: The contentious debate in Canada regarding the expansion of MAID (Medical Assistance in Dying) to include individuals whose sole underlying condition is a mental disorder. The government faced significant pushback from human rights groups and psychiatrists regarding the adequacy of existing mental health infrastructure.

key_events.description: Canadian Medical Association (CMA) expresses deep concern over readiness.
Special Joint Committee on Medical Assistance in Dying reports lack of consensus.
Government announces a delay in expansion implementation due to clinical safety concerns.

calibration_notes: Illustrates how professional opposition can reverse initial high support for progressive health policies.
